py-output.title: print and stdout
py-output.concept: `print` is the boundary between a Python calculation and judge-visible output. It converts each argument to text, inserts spaces between arguments, and ends with a newline by default, so exact stdout matters.

py-variables.title: Variables
py-variables.concept: A variable name is bound to an object by assignment. Rebinding the same name changes what future reads see, while any previous object keeps existing until no references need it.

py-numbers.title: Numbers
py-numbers.concept: Python integers have arbitrary precision, and numeric operators choose different meanings: `/` returns a float, `//` floors, `%` gives the remainder, and `**` powers. Problems often need the integer operators, not decimal division.

py-strings.title: Strings
py-strings.concept: A string is an immutable sequence of Unicode characters. Indexing reads one character, slicing reads a half-open range, and operations such as concatenation create a new string instead of editing the old one.
